info.title: How to install and deploy &suselinux; 16 with &agama;

abstract.para: &suselinux; 16 transitions from &yast; to the &agama; installer, introducing a purpose-built deployment model centered on modern hardware and improved automation. This chapter details these installer changes alongside updated procedures for accessing the recovery shell and registering systems against RMT infrastructure.

section.title: From &yast; to &agama;
section.para: Agama is available in three extractable image flavors:

listitem.para: full—complete copy of package repositories for all product variants

section.para: &agama; provides a Web-based front-end, a command-line interface, and an HTTP API. It supports unattended installation and offers a high degree of compatibility with &ay;'s file format and data schema. However, &agama; is not a drop-in replacement for &ay;: some profile adjustments are expected. A new, more powerful profile schema based on JSON/Jsonnet is also available.
Unlike &ay;, &agama; supports repeated configuration import. It is useful for iterative deployment workflows.
The installer medium now uses a standard live medium with dracut, &systemd; and &nm;, and provides a backward-compatibility layer with selected linuxrc options.

section.title: How to access the rescue system and recovery shell
section.para: &agama; installation images no longer ship with a separate rescue system. Until a dedicated rescue image is provided in a future release, you can access a recovery shell by:

listitem.para: Booting the installation image, switching to another virtual console, and logging in as &rootuser;
Booting the installation image and opening a terminal window
Appending 3 to the kernel command line before booting to start in runlevel 3, bypassing the graphical installer

section.title: Is the RMT server supported in SUSE Linux 16?
section.para: Yes, &suselinux; 16 instances can register against an RMT server for updates. However, the RMT service itself must currently run on a &slea; 15 instance, as RMT 15 is fully supported as an installation source for &suselinux; 16. RMT server support running directly on &suselinux; 16 is expected to be released with &suselinux; 16.1.
